hawkish_phrases: a number of reservations were expressed about individual elements of the proposed policy package
some concerns were voiced about a potential decline in the efficiency of the portfolio rebalancing channel and potential adverse side effects
A number of members assessed the case for renewed net asset purchases as not sufficiently strong
other members felt unable to support a cut of 10 basis points, as they were concerned about the possibility of increasingly adverse side effects
banks would benefit from TLTRO III, which offered them very favourable lending conditions
dovish_phrases: a comprehensive policy response was warranted to support the return to a sustained convergence path towards the Governing Council's medium-term inflation aim

the Governing Council was confronted with a protracted slowdown in the euro area economy, persistent downside risks and an inflation outlook that continued to fall short of its medium-term inflation aim
All members agreed that a further easing of the monetary policy stance was warranted
significant downward revisions to the inflation outlook had been observed, bringing the 2021 inflation projection down from 1.8% to 1.5%
inflation was moving further away from levels deemed consistent with the Governing Council's inflation aim
summary: The ECB cut the deposit rate by 10 basis points to -0.50%, restarted net asset purchases at €20 billion per month, improved TLTRO III conditions, and introduced a two-tier reserve system; while all members agreed easing was warranted, several members expressed reservations about the net asset purchase restart and some opposed even the 10bp rate cut due to side-effect concerns.
